content.problemStatement:
Given an integer NNN, help Chef in finding an NNN-digit odd positive integer\textbf{odd positive integer}odd positive integer XXX such that XXX is divisible by 333 but not by 999.

Note:\textbf{Note}:Note: There should not be any leading zeroes in XXX. In other words, 003003003 is not a valid 333-digit odd positive integer.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of testcases. The description of the TTT testcases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, denoting the number of digits in XXX.


Output Format
For each testcase, output a single line containing an NNN-digit odd positive integer XXX in decimal number system, such that XXX is divisible by 333 but not by 999.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111: 333 is the only 111-digit odd positive integer which is divisible by 333 but not by 999.
Test Case 222: 151515 is a 222-digit odd positive integer which is divisible by 333 but not by 999. 212121 is also a valid answer, among others. Note that 030303 is not a valid answer as there should not be any leading zeroes in the output.
